title: Long-form report
description: Data contract for the long-form report format: the canonical document format for audits, assessments, postmortems, due-diligence write-ups, security reviews, and root-cause analyses. Renders as a self-contained interactive HTML with a sticky top bar, compact hero, 3-column body (208px TOC | 1fr | 244px rail), stacked rounded section panels, a dark split-diff exhibit, and a collapsible appendix reference-index. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown in the top bar and footer, e.g. 'Operational Resilience Assessment' or {{REPORT_TYPE}}.

title.description: Main report title shown in the hero, e.g. 'Operational Resilience & Controls Assessment'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst shown in the hero-meta strip.

date.description: ISO date the report was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. The in-report switcher can change it live. Default for this format is 'purple'.

hero.description: The compact hero below the top bar — eyebrow, title, verdict badges, standfirst, and optional meta strip.

eyebrow.description: Tracked-uppercase mono eyebrow label above the title, e.g. 'Root Cause Analysis'.

title.description: Hero title (may differ from meta.title for line-break control). ≤72 characters.

standfirst.description: 2–3 sentence thesis/executive summary shown as hero-sum. States the conclusion first.

badges.description: Status/verdict/category pills shown below the title in the hero.

variant.description: Visual variant: 'verdict' (risk colour), 'cat' (accent colour), 'sev' (hold/amber colour), or 'default' (neutral).

metaItems.description: Optional key/value pairs shown in the hero-meta strip (thin row at the bottom of the hero). e.g. [{key:'Report ID', value:'OPS-RES-2026-Q2-047'}].

key.description: Label shown in small uppercase mono.

tocSections.description: Ordered list of section entries for the left-rail TOC scroll-spy index. Each entry maps to a section rendered in the main column. Supplement and Appendix entries are listed last.

id.description: Anchor id that matches the corresponding section's id attribute, e.g. 'context'.

num.description: Section number shown in mono in the TOC, e.g. '00' or 'App.' or 'Supp.'.

title.description: Section title shown next to the number in the TOC link.

sections.description: Ordered array of section panels rendered in the main article column. Each section becomes a .sec rounded panel with a banded header.

id.description: HTML id attribute for the section, used by TOC scroll-spy and anchor links, e.g. 'context'.

title.description: Section heading shown in the banded header H2.

folded.description: If true, the section ships as a collapsible <details> (used for Supplement and Appendix). Defaults to false.

iconCategory.description: Icon shown in the section header band, drawn from the house SVG set.

blocks.description: Content blocks inside the section, rendered in order. Each block has a 'kind' that controls how it is rendered.

kind.description: Block type: 'prose' = a paragraph or paragraphs; 'kv-grid' = 4-column key/value grid; 'phen-rows' = phenomenon/labelled-fact rows; 'cause-chain' = numbered vertical stepper; 'fix-card' = finding/fix card with optional diff; 'findings-accordion' = collapsible finding rows; 'data-table' = 2px-rule data table; 'risk-list' = id'd risk list.

text.description: For kind='prose': the paragraph HTML/text content.

items.description: For list-type blocks: the rows or entries.

railFacts.description: Metadata cards shown in the sticky right rail. Each card has a header and a list of key/value fact rows.

label.description: Card header label shown in the banded rail-card-hd strip, e.g. 'Report metadata'.

facts.description: Key/value rows inside the rail card.

value.description: Fact value shown in dd. May include a status token name prefixed with 'st:' (e.g. 'st:risk:Non-conformant') to render as a .st-badge.

findings.description: Optional array of detailed findings for the findings-accordion section. Each entry maps to a collapsible <details> finding row.

id.description: Evidence ID, e.g. 'EV-DOC-001'. Shown in the mono fi-code chip.

type.description: Finding type label shown in the fi-type chip, e.g. 'Control', 'Data', 'Telemetry'.

title.description: One-line finding title shown in the collapsible summary row.

confidenceLabel.description: Text for the confidence badge, e.g. 'Confidence · High'.

body.description: Prose explanation shown in the expanded find-body, in plain text or light HTML.

evidence.description: Optional verbatim code/log excerpt shown in a mono evi-body-card block.

references.description: Evidence ledger entries for the collapsible Appendix section. Every EV-* id referenced in the body should have a row here.

id.description: Stable evidence ID, e.g. 'EV-DOC-001'. Used as the HTML anchor id (id='ev-EV-DOC-001').

type.description: Evidence type shown in the Type column, e.g. 'Control', 'Source', 'Data', 'Telemetry'.

where.description: Source locator shown in the Locator column (mono), e.g. 'catalogue://controls/OR-CTL-118'.

brief.description: One-sentence précis of the evidence shown in the Précis column.

linkKind.description: Optional link-badge type for the external-source icon. Maps to .evi-link-icon--<kind>.

hero.title: Idempotency gap in the settlement orchestrator — duplicate disbursements under retry
hero.standfirst: A post-commit / pre-persist gap in the settlement orchestrator's idempotency key write allowed retried requests to execute a second mutation when downstream latency exceeded the client retry window. Four confirmed duplicate disbursements were produced over the review period; all were detected by reconciliation and reversed. The primary fix closes the gap by writing the key atomically inside the commit transaction.

items.label: Root cause
items.value: Post-commit key write (non-atomic) opens duplicate window

items.headline: Idempotency key written after commit
items.detail: The orchestrator calls ledger.Commit() then separately idem.Put() — two operations with no shared transaction.

items.headline: Partial-failure window exposed
items.detail: If the process restarts or the client retries before Put() completes, the dedup lookup returns MISS.

items.headline: Retry processes as new request
items.detail: The retried request passes the dedup check, commits a second mutation, and returns HTTP 200.

items.text: Duplicate window remains open until the primary fix ships. Interim mitigation: reconciliation alert on same-day duplicate request_id.
Unique-index hardening requires a clean backfill; run the dedup report at full history before migrating.
Transfer orchestrator shares the post-commit key-write pattern — must be remediated together.

sections.title: Lateral exposure — same pattern, other paths

findings.title: OR-CTL-118 mandates exactly-once for externally-visible mutations, with the idempotency key written atomically with the mutation
findings.body: The control catalogue defines OR-CTL-118 as a closed requirement: every externally-visible state mutation must be exactly-once under client retry, and the deduplication key must be written within the same transaction boundary as the mutation it guards.
findings.evidence:
OR-CTL-118  Exactly-once for externally-visible mutations
  Mechanism : A deduplication key MUST be persisted within the SAME transaction boundary as the mutation.

findings.title: Reconciliation found 4 ledger pairs sharing one request id — confirmed duplicate disbursements
findings.body: A dedup query over the review window returned four cases where two ledger_entries rows carry the same (account_id, request_id) with non-overlapping ids and timestamps 0.4–4.8s apart.
findings.evidence:
SELECT account_id, request_id, count(*), max(ts)-min(ts) AS gap
FROM ledger_entries
WHERE created_at >= '2026-04-01' GROUP BY 1,2 HAVING count(*) > 1;
 -- 4 rows returned

references.brief: OR-CTL-118: exactly-once for externally-visible mutations; key written atomically.
handleDisbursement: ledger.Commit() then separate idem.Put() — non-atomic key write.
Idempotency store uses a distinct connection; Put and Commit cannot share a transaction.
Four ledger pairs share one request id; all four reconciled as breaks and reversed.
Trace of the reproduced case: first commit, client retry +0.41s, dedup miss, second commit.
Orchestrator log showing dedup MISS on the retry and the second commit ok status=200.
